summary_one_line: Input IHDR color_type byte at offset 0x19 must equal 0x03 (PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE) to take the winning true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The PNG IHDR color_type field (the byte at file offset 0x19, immediately after the 1-byte bit-depth) must hold the exact value 0x03, which is PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE; only then does the branch compare png_ptr->color_type == PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE succeed and the true side dispatch into png_init_palette_transformations.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All ten winner-resolving (cmplog) seeds carry 0x03 at offset 0x19 (BYTE DIFF shows 03(.)x10), so every winning input declares a palette image; per-role coverage confirms the true side and png_init_palette_transformations fire only in W.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking (naive) seeds never put 0x03 at offset 0x19 — they hold 0x00 (grayscale) or 0x04 (gray+alpha) — so color_type != PALETTE, the false branch is taken and png_init_rgb_transformations runs instead.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace intercepts the integer CMP color_type == 3, learns the constant 3 operand, and substitutes it at the matching input byte (offset 0x19); naive has only blind havoc and cannot reliably land the single exact constant 0x03 on that one structurally-gated byte.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is an equality test of png_ptr->color_type against PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE, true side globally blocked.
All ten winner seeds hold 0x03 at offset 0x19 while losers hold 0x00 or 0x04, the PNG color_type field.
The true side and palette-init path execute only in the winner-resolving seed set.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: T=10 F=0  T=0 F=10  if (png_ptr->color_type == PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE)

evidence_trail.claim: The winner enters png_init_palette_transformations and the loser enters png_init_rgb_transformations instead.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which finds CMP operands in the input and substitutes the other operand at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has only havoc + token mutators and no I2S substitution.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 3912 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 282c4c4b85d3af6c` and observed 1 I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 3 of the ancestor chain, the lower-bound signal of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current cmplog build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on color_type==3 where naive resolves the true branch as fast as cmplog, showing the single exact-constant byte is not what cmplog's I2S substitution is buying.

weakest_evidence_point: The dash-row I2S-floor signal is a lower bound and indirect — it confirms an I2S find in the chain but does not prove the specific 0x03 substitution at offset 0x19 was the I2S edit rather than a fortunate havoc mutation in a later generation.
